sections.paragraph:
fut. יִסְמֹךְ-

(1) to place, or lay something upon any thing, so that it may rest upon, and be supported by it. סָמַךְ יָד עַל to lay the hand on any thing (die Hand worauf ftufüßen), so as to lean upon the hand, Exo 29:10, 15 Exo 29:15, 19 Exo 29:19 Lev 1:4 3:2 8:14 , etc. Amo 5:19, וְסָמַךְ יָדֹו עַל־הַקִּיר “and leaneth his hand upon the wall.” Intr. to rest upon any thing. Psa 88:8, עָלַי סָֽמְכָה חֲמָתֶךָ “thy wrath resteth upon me.”

(2) to uphold, to sustain, to aid (Eth. ሰመከ፡) followed by an acc. Psa 37:17, 24 Psa 37:24 54:6 . Eze 30:6, סֹמְכֵי מִצְרַיִם “those who help Egypt,” the allies of Egypt; followed by לְ Psa 145:14. Part. pass. סָמוּךְ propped, upheld, i.e. unmoved, firm, Psa 112:8 Isa 26:3. Followed by two acc. to sustain one with any thing, i.e. to bestow upon him liberally. Gen 27:37, דָּגָן וְתִירשׁ סְמַכְתִּיו “I have sustained him with corn and new wine.” Psa 51:14.

(3) to approach, followed by אֶל Eze 24:2. (Syr. id. The signification of approaching is derived from that of resting upon, being contiguous; comp. Rabb. סמד to cohere, to be connected, near, סמיד near).

Niphal, to be propped, supported, Jud. 16:29 to stay oneself, to rest upon, Psa 71:6 Isa 48:2. Metaph. 2Ch 32:8.

Piel, to stay, i.e. to refresh, Son 2:5 (compare סָעַד). Hence pr.n. יִסְמַכְיָהוּ. and-
